name: Minho Coast
tagline: Wild Atlantic coastline meets lush green valleys
description: Portugal's greenest corner — dramatic cliffs, wild beaches, and ancient vineyards stretching to the Spanish border.
whyDescription: The Minho is Portugal at its most elemental — rugged Atlantic coastline, impossibly green hillsides draped in vineyards, and medieval towns that feel entirely untouched by mass tourism. This is the home of Vinho Verde, produced from grapes grown on the granite slopes above the Lima and Minho rivers.
highlights: Wild Atlantic beaches and dramatic sea cliffs
Vinho Verde wine country and river valleys
Medieval towns and romanesque monasteries
National Parks and ancient Roman roads
Authentic village life far from the tourist trail
howToGetHere: Fly into Porto (OPO), then 45–90 minutes north by car. The A28 runs the length of the coast.
bestTimeToVisit: May–September for beach weather; March–May for lush landscapes and fewer crowds.
whatToExpect: A slower pace, genuine hospitality, and landscapes that surprise at every turn. Pack layers — the Atlantic keeps things fresh even in summer.

insiderRecommendations.name: Afife Beach
insiderRecommendations.description: One of the last untouched beaches on the coast — no hotels, no beach bars, just dunes and Atlantic swells.

insiderRecommendations.name: Quinta da Calçada
insiderRecommendations.description: Small family estate producing exceptional Loureiro. Ask for a barrel tasting if you visit in spring.

insiderRecommendations.name: Caminha market
insiderRecommendations.description: Saturday morning market in a medieval square. The local cheese and presunto are extraordinary.

seoDescription: Exclusive villa rentals on Portugal's Minho Coast — wild Atlantic beaches, Vinho Verde country, and unspoiled medieval villages.

name: Porto & Douro
tagline: Baroque city grandeur meets world-class wine country
description: A UNESCO city of bridges, azulejos, and port wine cellars, with the spectacular Douro Valley terraces stretching east.
whyDescription: Porto is one of Europe's great cities — fiercely proud, architecturally magnificent, and utterly unpretentious. The Douro Valley, stretching 100km east, is a UNESCO World Heritage landscape of terraced vineyards carved into schist hillsides over two millennia. Together they form Portugal's most rewarding region for a slow, immersive stay.
highlights: UNESCO-listed historic centre and iconic Dom Luís bridge
Port wine lodges and tastings in Vila Nova de Gaia
Douro Valley river cruises and quinta visits
World-class restaurants and a thriving food scene
Atlantic beaches 20 minutes from the city centre
howToGetHere: Porto Francisco Sá Carneiro Airport (OPO) — 20 minutes to the city centre by metro (Violeta line).
bestTimeToVisit: April–June and September–October offer ideal weather and fewer crowds. September–October coincides with the Douro grape harvest.
whatToExpect: A city that rewards wandering — get lost in the Ribeira, cross the bridge on foot, and eat a francesinha at a neighbourhood café.

insiderRecommendations.name: Livraria Lello
insiderRecommendations.description: One of the world's most beautiful bookshops. Go early morning to beat the queues.

insiderRecommendations.name: Quinta do Crasto
insiderRecommendations.description: Outstanding Douro estate with a stunning infinity pool overlooking the river. Book the harvest lunch in October.

insiderRecommendations.name: Cantinho do Avillez
insiderRecommendations.description: José Avillez's Porto outpost — refined Portuguese cooking without the formality of a tasting menu.

seoTitle: Porto & Douro Valley Villas | Portugal Active
seoDescription: Luxury villa rentals in Porto and the Douro Valley — UNESCO heritage, world-class wine, and Portugal's most spectacular river landscape.

name: Algarve
tagline: Golden cliffs, secret sea caves, and endless sunshine
description: Portugal's sun-drenched south — iconic limestone sea stacks, hidden coves, championship golf, and 300 days of sunshine.
whyDescription: The Algarve is Portugal's most celebrated coastline — and for good reason. Sculpted by millennia of Atlantic erosion, the western Sotavento coast delivers some of the most dramatic cliff scenery in Europe. But look beyond the famous postcards and you'll find a region of genuine depth: pristine natural parks, Moorish whitewashed villages, and a culinary tradition built on the freshest seafood in the country.
highlights: Iconic limestone sea stacks and golden cliff formations
Hidden sea caves accessible by kayak or boat
Ria Formosa Natural Park — a 60km lagoon and island system
Championship golf courses in a Mediterranean climate
Outstanding seafood restaurants and local fish markets
howToGetHere: Faro Airport (FAO) serves the eastern Algarve. For the western coast, Lagos is 90 minutes from Faro by car or train.
bestTimeToVisit: May–June and September–October are ideal — warm, sunny, and significantly quieter than peak summer.
whatToExpect: Expect warm evenings, cold Atlantic swells (even in summer), and the best grilled fish of your life.

insiderRecommendations.name: Praia do Camilo
insiderRecommendations.description: A short staircase descent to a pocket cove framed by golden rock. Arrive early — it holds perhaps 50 people comfortably.

insiderRecommendations.name: A Forja, Lagos
insiderRecommendations.description: No-frills local restaurant serving the freshest cataplana in the western Algarve. Cash only.

insiderRecommendations.name: Sagres Point at sunset
insiderRecommendations.description: The southwestern tip of continental Europe. The scale of the Atlantic from here is humbling.

seoDescription: Curated villa rentals on the Algarve — golden sea cliffs, hidden coves, and 300 days of Portuguese sunshine.

name: Lisbon
tagline: Seven hills, seven centuries of history
description: Europe's westernmost capital — Moorish hilltop castles, art nouveau cafés, and the melancholy beauty of fado echoing through tiled alleyways.
whyDescription: Lisbon rewards those who wander without a plan — take the vintage tram up to Alfama, sit in a miradouro at sunset with a glass of wine, and let the city unfold at its own pace.
highlights: Alfama and Mouraria — medieval Moorish quarters
Belém Tower and the monuments to the Age of Discovery
World-class contemporary art at MAAT
Sintra palaces and Cascais coast 30 minutes away
Vibrant food and nightlife scene
seoTitle: Lisbon Villas Coming Soon | Portugal Active
seoDescription: Curated luxury stays in Lisbon — coming soon from Portugal Active.

name: Alentejo
tagline: Endless plains, ancient cork oaks, and slow time
description: Portugal's vast interior heartland — rolling plains studded with cork oaks, medieval walled towns, and the country's most acclaimed red wines.
whyDescription: The Alentejo is Portugal at its most meditative — vast skies, golden light, and a pace of life that invites you to slow down completely.
highlights: UNESCO-listed Évora and its Roman temple
World-class Alentejo red wines and olive oils
Ancient megalithic monuments and prehistoric sites
Herdade agri-tourism estates and farm-to-table dining
Untouched Alentejo coast — wild, windswept, and crowd-free
seoTitle: Alentejo Villas Coming Soon | Portugal Active
seoDescription: Luxury stays in the Alentejo — coming soon from Portugal Active.

name: Brazil
tagline: Jungle, coast, and the rhythm of a continent
description: From the Amazon to the Cerrado, from the Northeast coast to the Pantanal — Brazil's scale and diversity defy easy description.
whyDescription: Brazil is not a country — it's a continent. Portugal Active is curating a select collection of exceptional stays across Brazil's most compelling destinations.
highlights: Amazon rainforest lodges and river expeditions
Northeast coast beaches — Trancoso, Arraial d'Ajuda, Jericoacoara
Pantanal wildlife safaris
Iguaçu Falls and the Atlantic Forest
Lençóis Maranhenses — the white sand dune desert
seoTitle: Brazil Stays Coming Soon | Portugal Active
seoDescription: Curated luxury stays across Brazil — coming soon from Portugal Active.
